title: Senior Scientist (Protein Analysis unit)
description:
Senior Scientist, Protein Analysis

We are seeking a Senior Scientist with deep expertise in protein analysis to lead antibody characterization across discovery and optimization, from high-throughput screening through developability. Reporting to the Research Manager, you will drive assay development and data generation, collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, and contribute to novel protein analysis technology development. After initial training, you are expected to independently manage antibody analysis.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead antibody binding affinity screening using SPR.
  • Plan and execute antibody/ligand binding analyses.
  • Plan and conduct biophysical analyses to evaluate physicochemical properties of antibodies.
  • Assess developability and manufacturability of antibody drug candidates.
  • Support projects from high-throughput screening to late-stage developability assessment, collaborating with multidisciplinary teams to achieve project milestones.
  • Contribute to the development and implementation of novel protein analysis technologies.

Required Qualifications

  • Ph.D. in Biophysics, Biochemistry, Structural Biology, Biomedical Science, Chemical Biology, Biotechnology, Life Science, or Pharmaceutical Science, or a related scientific field with 0 – 2+ years of relevant industry or research experience, preferably in monoclonal antibody drug discovery.
  • Strong expertise in protein-protein interaction analysis, binding kinetic and affinity analysis, physicochemical characterization of monoclonal antibodies.
  • Hands-on experience in at least one of the following areas:

o   Protein–protein interaction analysis (SPR, BLI, GCI)
o   Column chromatography (ion-exchange, size-exclusion, reversed-phase)
o   Biophysical characterization (DLS, DSC, solubility, viscosity, stability testing)
o   Protein LC–MS analysis

  • Ability to work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment
  • Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently and in team settings
  • Proficiency in spoken and written English.

The ideal candidate

  • Demonstrates a strong passion for medical science and drug innovation.
  • Capable of independently running and troubleshooting assays, optimizing methods, and adopting new technologies.
  • Ensures data integrity, safety, and compliance with SOPs.
  • Skilled in data analysis, visualization, and clear communication to diverse audiences.
  • Effective in planning, meeting deadlines, and collaborating across teams.

Application procedures

Interested applicants are invited to email their detailed resume, qualifications, availability, current and expected salary to: recruitment@chugai-pharmabody.com

For more information regarding our Company, you may refer to our website at:
http://chugai-pharmabody.com/

We regret that only short-listed candidates will be notified

postedCompany.description: Chugai Pharmabody Research Pte. Ltd. (“CPR”) was established in Singapore as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Chugai Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd (Chugai), a member of Roche group, in January 2012 and started operations on July 2, 2012. Located at Biopolis in Singapore, it aims to generate new antibody drugs based on Chugai’s proprietary antibody engineering technologies, such as recycling antibody®, sweeping antibody®, bispecific antibody, various Fc engineered antibodies, and other new antibody engineering technologies that are under development.
postedCompany.name: CHUGAI PHARMABODY RESEARCH PTE. LTD.
